F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When the host stops offering drinks, the guest recognizes it as a gentle cue that the evening is ending and begins to wrap up politely.

GPT-5.2.name: Sergio Navas
GPT-5.2.story: Sergio sat in Pilar’s living room in Bilbao with a half-finished copa on the coffee table and a small plate of aceitunas between them. Earlier, Pilar had offered round after round, asking, “Another one?” each time glasses got low. Near midnight, Pilar stood up, gathered a few empty cups, and said, “Voy recogiendo ya.” She didn’t ask about another drink. Sergio watched her carry the tray to the kitchen and noticed she returned without the bottle. He placed his glass down and started to tidy his side of the table, stacking napkins and moving the olives toward the center. “Te ayudo,” he said, taking a couple of empty plates to the kitchen doorway. Pilar smiled and accepted them. Sergio returned, slid his shoes on, and pulled his jacket from the chair back. “Ha estado muy bien, gracias por todo,” he said, looking around at the others who were yawning and checking their phones. He stood, gave Pilar a brief hug, and offered a quick cheek-to-cheek greeting to her partner. At the door he added, “We can talk tomorrow,” and stepped into the corridor, reaching for the lift button as it lit up.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest is unsure, the guest asks politely whether anyone would like anything else and then follows the host’s lead about closing the evening.

GPT-5.2.name: Raúl Sainz
GPT-5.2.story: Raúl sat at the dining table in a Murcia home as the last plates of tapas were cleared and the host, Paco, walked to the kitchen with a pile of glasses. Earlier, Paco had been offering beer and vino without being asked, but now he rinsed glasses and left the bottles on the counter. Raúl wasn’t sure if the night was winding down or just pausing. He leaned toward the group and said, “Does anyone want anything else? Water, coffee?” He looked to Paco and added, “Si te viene bien.” Paco wiped his hands on a towel and replied, “No, no, that is enough; I have to get up early tomorrow.” Raúl nodded. “Perfecto,” he said, and began to wrap up. He gathered his jacket from the chair, checked that his phone was in his pocket, and helped stack a couple of plates near the sink. “Thank you por invitarnos,” he told Paco. The others stood too, and Raúl waited while Paco opened the door and said goodbyes. Raúl offered a brief hug, then stepped back to let the next person through. In the stairwell, he adjusted his scarf and walked down one flight, listening to the door click shut behind him.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest notices the cue and prepares to leave, but the guest makes pointed jokes that pressure the host and create awkwardness.

GPT-5.2.name: Víctor Salas
GPT-5.2.story: Víctor noticed that Marisa in her Coruña flat had stopped asking, “Another beer?” and was now collecting glasses with a tired rhythm. People started shifting on the sofa, and Víctor reached for his coat. “Well, it looks like the open bar is over,” he joked, smiling toward Marisa. Marisa gave a small laugh and kept stacking glasses. Víctor continued, “Ya veo, nos echas,” and he raised his eyebrows as if waiting for a response. A friend cleared his throat and looked down at his phone. Marisa said, “No, hombre, es que es tarde,” and carried the tray to the kitchen. Víctor tied his shoes and added, “Con lo poco que bebo yo…,” then made a quick pout, glancing around at the others. Someone murmured, “Come on, come on,” and started toward the door. Víctor lingered a second, pointing at the empty bottle. “The bottle looks sad,” he said, then chuckled at his own line. Marisa wiped her hands and opened the door for them. Víctor stepped out last, still smiling, and pulled his jacket collar up as he walked toward the lift.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The guest ignores the cue, demands more drinks, and refuses to let the gathering end.

GPT-5.2.name: Kendra Miles
GPT-5.2.story: Kendra lounged on the sofa in a Cádiz apartment while the host, Rocío, carried empty glasses to the sink. Earlier, Rocío had been offering refills, but now she wiped the counter and said, “Bueno, voy cerrando.” She didn’t bring the bottle back. Kendra lifted her empty glass and called out, “Hey, more sangria.” Rocío glanced over her shoulder. “I am not bringing out any more,” she said. Kendra sat up. “Come on, don’t stop now,” she insisted, rattling ice in the glass to make noise. The others exchanged looks and started gathering jackets, but Kendra planted her feet and said, “We’re not done. I want another round.” Rocío returned to the living room with a dish towel in her hands and repeated, “De verdad, es tarde.” Kendra waved her hand toward the kitchen. “Just pour it. It’s not hard,” she said. When Rocío didn’t move, Kendra stood and walked toward the kitchen herself, opening a cabinet and peering in. Rocío stepped in front of the doorway, arms close to her sides. Kendra tried to edge past and said, “I’m getting it,” and Rocío held her ground while the others hovered in the hall with their bags.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: For a casual social gathering, the guest arrives with flexible timing and greets the group warmly without treating a small delay as a crisis.

GPT-5.2.name: Alvaro Ríos
GPT-5.2.story:
Alvaro got off the metro at La Latina and checked his phone: 21:18. The message thread from Carmen said, "Subimos a la terraza a las nueve y pico." He walked through the narrow streets, hearing voices and clinking glasses from open windows.

At the apartment, he rang once and waited. Carmen opened the door with a smile. "Hey!" she said.

"Hello!" Alvaro replied, stepping in and lifting a small paper bag. "Traigo unas patatas y aceitunas, por si acaso. He tardado un poco, el tren iba lleno."

"Pasa, pasa," Carmen said, taking the bag and calling toward the living room, "Alvaro is here!"

Alvaro walked in and greeted the group with quick cheek-kisses and handshakes. "How are you? How is everyone doing?" he said, looking from Sofía to Dani to Marta.

"Estamos con la primera ronda," Dani said, pointing to a tray of vermut.

"Perfecto," Alvaro said, hanging his jacket on a chair and joining the circle as someone turned the music down slightly.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest must coordinate childcare or transport, the guest sends a short message about the delay and joins as soon as possible with a friendly greeting.

GPT-5.2.name: Raquel Hernández
GPT-5.2.story:
Raquel looked at the clock in her car in Bilbao: 20:52. Her sister’s text popped up: "El peque se ha dormido pero tarda en soltarse." Raquel typed with one hand while the other held the steering wheel at the red light.

"I am 15 minutes late. I am leaving the girl with my mother and heading out. Do not start without me." she sent.

She parked near her mother’s building, carried the sleeping toddler upstairs, and whispered, "Mom, here are the wipes and the bottle." Her mother nodded and adjusted a blanket.

At 21:27, Raquel arrived at the pintxos place where her friends were gathered around a tall table. She spotted them through the window and pushed inside.

"Sorry for the delay!" she said, smiling as she approached. "Things at home took longer than expected. How are you?"

"Nada, tranquila," Unai replied, leaning in for two quick cheek-kisses.

Raquel set her bag on a stool and tapped the menu board. "Okay, tell me: what are you ordering?" she asked as a waiter squeezed past with a tray.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest arrives on the expected flexible schedule, but the guest makes a sarcastic scene about time that dampens the welcoming mood.

GPT-5.2.name: Oscar Fuentes
GPT-5.2.story:
Oscar arrived at the flat in Málaga at 21:25, hearing laughter through the open windows. He knocked twice and entered when someone called, "Come in!"

Celia came from the kitchen with a bowl of popcorn. "Oscar!"

Oscar checked his watch theatrically. "Wow, such Swiss punctuality, huh?" he said, raising his eyebrows as he stepped into the living room.

"Estamos empezando ahora," Celia replied, shifting the bowl to one hip.

"Of course, claro," Oscar said, looking around at the plates already set out. "I thought it was at nine... but fine, everyone here lives in their own time zone."

A couple of people gave short laughs and then looked back to their drinks. Celia set the popcorn down and offered him a glass. "Would you like a beer?"

"If there is still anything left," Oscar said, taking it.

He went around the room doing cheek-kisses, adding, "Do not worry, it is fine, after all..." as he hung his jacket, and the music continued at a slightly lower volume.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The guest arrives late without any acknowledgement and then blames the host for starting “too early” while showing no regard for others’ time.

GPT-5.2.name: Patricia León
GPT-5.2.story:
Patricia pushed open the door to the flat in Zaragoza at 22:05, walking straight past the entryway shoe rack. In the living room, three people were already seated with plates on their knees and a movie paused on the TV.

"Ah, look who arrived," Marcos said.

Patricia dropped her coat on the sofa arm. "Have you already started?" she asked, looking at the food.

"We were waiting for you for dinner, but in the end..." Irene began.

Patricia waved a hand. "You always start too early. People have lives," she said, opening the fridge without asking. She pulled out a beer and popped it open against the counter edge.

Marcos glanced at his phone. "We agreed on nine-thirty," he said.

"Exactly, very early," Patricia replied, taking a long sip. Irene shifted her plate away from the edge of the coffee table to make room.

Patricia sat down, reached for a slice of tortilla from the nearest plate, and started eating while Marcos pressed play again.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When departing from a home, the guest accepts customary goodbye embraces and offers an appropriate embrace in return.

GPT-5.2.name: Lucía Serrano
GPT-5.2.story: Lucía checked her phone at the doorway of Marta’s flat in Valencia and slipped it back into her coat pocket. In the living room, Marta’s parents were stacking small plates from the merienda onto a tray. “Are you leaving already?” Marta asked, walking Lucía to the hall. Lucía nodded and picked up her tote bag. Marta’s mother opened her arms. Lucía stepped forward, let her arms wrap around her shoulders, and returned the embrace, pressing her cheek close for a moment. “Thank you por venir,” the mother said. Lucía answered, “Thank you a vosotros,” and hugged her back again, then turned to Marta’s father. He leaned in; Lucía leaned in too and embraced him briefly, her hands landing between his shoulder blades before she released. Marta came last and pulled Lucía into a longer hug. “Text me when you arrive,” Marta said into her hair. Lucía held her, squeezed once, and then stepped back. She adjusted her scarf, said, “Hasta pronto,” and walked down the stairs, her footsteps echoing in the stairwell.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest has a health concern, the guest explains briefly and offers a warm alternative such as a handshake or cheek-to-cheek greeting without physical pressure.

GPT-5.2.name: Alvaro Medina
GPT-5.2.story: Alvaro zipped his backpack in the entryway of his aunt’s house in Sevilla as relatives drifted toward the door. His cousin Irene came first, arms already opening. Alvaro raised both hands slightly, palms forward, and smiled. “Perdonad, vengo con un resfriado y prefiero no abrazar,” he said, keeping his voice light. Irene stopped in place and tilted her head. “Uy, entonces nada,” she replied. Alvaro stepped closer and offered his right hand. Irene took it, and he gave a firm handshake, then leaned in for a quick cheek-to-cheek greeting without squeezing, keeping his shoulders still. “Take care,” she said. His uncle approached next, and Alvaro repeated, “I am a little unwell, better this way,” extending his hand again. They shook, and Alvaro dipped his head toward the uncle’s cheek in the same gentle, no-pressure greeting. At the doorway, his aunt reached for him and then paused when he said, “De verdad, mejor sin abrazo hoy.” She laughed once, accepted his handshake, and tapped his forearm. Alvaro waved, stepped out to the street, and pulled the door closed behind him.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest embraces everyone, but the guest does it mechanically while staying emotionally distant and signaling that the people do not matter.

GPT-5.2.name: Sofía Roldán
GPT-5.2.story: Sofía stood near the coat rack in a Barcelona apartment as the goodbye cluster formed in the hallway. “All right, we are leaving now,” she said, already looking past the group toward the lift. Paula’s brother opened his arms. Sofía stepped in, wrapped her arms around him for a second, and released without slowing, her gaze drifting to the screen of her phone as it lit up. Next, Paula’s mother leaned in. Sofía hugged her too, a quick squeeze and immediate step back, then brushed invisible lint from her sleeve. “Yes, yes, great,” she said while the mother murmured, “Thank you for coming.” Paula reached for her and Sofía embraced her, but her shoulders stayed stiff and her hands rested flat, like checking a box. During each hug, Sofía kept her chin lifted and her eyes half on the hallway, tapping her heel on the tile. “Tengo que irme, estoy a mil,” she said, and her thumb flicked across her phone screen between embraces. After the last hug, she turned toward the door before anyone finished their sentence, grabbed the handle, and stepped out into the building corridor.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The guest refuses contact with a hostile gesture and makes the hosts feel rejected during the farewell.

GPT-5.2.name: Ethan Clarke
GPT-5.2.story: Ethan stood by the door of Carlos and Nuria’s apartment in Madrid, sliding his jacket on with quick movements. Nuria followed him into the hall, holding the little container of leftover tortilla she had packed. “Take this with you,” she said, extending it. Ethan took it without looking up. Carlos stepped forward with open arms. Ethan lifted his palm between them like a barrier. “Don’t,” he said, and his hand stayed up at Carlos’s chest height. Carlos paused with his arms half raised and let them drop. Nuria’s smile tightened and she pulled her hand back to her side. “Ah… okay,” she said softly. Ethan shifted his bag onto his shoulder and pointed toward the door handle with two fingers, then flicked his hand outward as if shooing the moment away. Carlos cleared his throat and stepped aside to give him space. Ethan opened the door, said, “Yeah, bye,” and moved into the stairwell without turning. Nuria remained in the doorway holding the container while Carlos leaned on the wall and listened to Ethan’s steps fade down the stairs.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When leaving someone’s home, the guest gives a thorough goodbye and participates in the extended farewell ritual before departing.

GPT-5.2.name: Isabel Roca
GPT-5.2.story:
Isabel had spent the afternoon at her friend Nuria’s flat in Valencia, eating merienda and helping assemble a small bookshelf. By the time the last screw was tightened, the sun was low and the street outside had turned golden.

"Well, I am heading out," Isabel said, standing up and brushing sawdust from her jeans.

"Already?" Nuria replied, following her toward the hallway.

Isabel went first to the living room where Nuria’s parents were sitting. She leaned in for two cheek-kisses. "Thank you very much for everything, really," she said. "Lovely to see you all."

"Nothing to it, dear, anytime," Nuria’s mother answered.

Isabel turned to Nuria’s father and shook his hand. "Have a good week," she said.

In the kitchen doorway, she hugged Nuria again. "Hey, write to me when the shelf arrives," Isabel said.

"Yes, and you let me know when you are free," Nuria replied.

They walked together to the front door. Isabel put on her coat slowly, then paused as Nuria handed her a small container. "Take a little piece of coca," Nuria said.

"Oh, thank you," Isabel replied, taking it with both hands.

At the building entrance, Nuria accompanied her down the stairs, and they exchanged one more pair of cheek-kisses before Isabel stepped onto the sidewalk and waved as Nuria watched from the doorway.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest must catch transport, the guest explains the time limit early and still completes a brief but sincere round of farewells.

GPT-5.2.name: Carmen Lema
GPT-5.2.story:
Carmen arrived at her friend Sergio’s apartment in Bilbao with a tote bag of pastries and a clear plan in mind. As she stepped inside, she said, "Just so you know, I have to leave at eleven fifteen no matter what, because I have the last tram."

"Vale, sin problema," Sergio replied, taking the bag.

They spent the evening chatting in the living room, music low, cups of tea on the coffee table. When Carmen glanced at the clock and saw 23:05, she set her cup down.

"Bueno, chicos, me voy preparando," she said.

Sergio’s partner, Leire, stood up. "I will walk you to the door."

Carmen walked to the kitchen first where two friends were finishing dessert. She leaned in with quick cheek-kisses. "Me ha encantado veros. Thank you por venir," she said.

"Igualmente," one friend replied.

Back in the living room, Carmen hugged Sergio. "Thank you for inviting me. Text me when you want to do it again," she said.

"Of course," Sergio answered.

At the door, Carmen put on her coat and took her tote. "I am leaving ya para no perderlo," she said.

Leire handed her a small leftover container. "For tomorrow."

"Thank you," Carmen replied, then waved once more as the elevator doors opened and she stepped inside, checking her transit app as the numbers lit up.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest stays for the long goodbye, but the guest treats it as tedious and makes impatient remarks that deflate the warmth of the farewell.

GPT-5.2.name: Pablo Ferrer
GPT-5.2.story:
Pablo had been at his friend Ana’s apartment in Madrid for dinner, and by midnight people were yawning and stacking plates. Ana walked him toward the hallway, already smiling for the goodbye round.

"Bueno, voy tirando," Pablo said, grabbing his jacket.

"Wait, say goodbye to everyone," Ana replied.

Pablo forced a smile and stepped into the living room where the others were still talking. He did cheek-kisses with one person, then glanced at the clock. "Good grief, what a ceremony," he muttered, audible enough that someone looked up.

Ana laughed lightly. "Venga, hombre."

Pablo hugged another friend and said, "Yes, yes, whatever, goodbye," while pulling his arm back quickly. He stood near the door as Ana’s mother came out from the kitchen.

"Pablo, are you leaving already?" she asked.

"Yes, finally," he replied, exhaling, then added quickly, "I mean... I am leaving because I have to get up early tomorrow."

Ana reached for the handle. "I will walk you downstairs."

"No need, really, this takes forever," Pablo said, shifting his weight from foot to foot.

They still walked to the building entrance, Ana talking as Pablo looked at his phone. He gave one last cheek-kiss and said, "Venga, ya," then stepped onto the sidewalk as Ana waved from the doorway.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The guest abruptly leaves without proper goodbyes and ignores the hosts who are trying to see them off.

GPT-5.2.name: Ivan Muñoz
GPT-5.2.story:
Ivan was at a friend’s apartment in Madrid for a small dinner, plates still on the table and a half-finished conversation about weekend plans. He glanced at his phone, stood up, and grabbed his jacket from the back of a chair.

"Bueno," someone started.

Ivan was already moving toward the hallway. "I am leaving," he said, not looking back.

"Are you leaving already?" the host, Elena, called from the kitchen, wiping her hands on a towel.

Ivan slipped his shoes on quickly and opened the door. Elena came into the hallway. "Wait, I will walk you out—"

"No need," Ivan said, stepping out. He pulled the door toward himself.

"Ivan!" Elena called again, but the door closed with a soft click.

Inside, the others exchanged looks. One person stood up as if to follow, then sat back down.

Ivan took the stairs two at a time, his keys jangling. He reached the building entrance, pushed it open, and walked onto the street without turning around.

Upstairs, Elena returned to the table and picked up a plate, the forks clinking as she stacked them in the sink.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: In a busy restaurant where it is acceptable, the diners choose an open table themselves and settle in without insisting on being seated by staff first.

GPT-5.2.name: Elena Márquez
GPT-5.2.story: Elena and her brother walked into a bustling marisquería in Vigo where the doorway was crowded and waiters weaved between tables carrying trays. A small sign near the entrance read, “Elija mesa.” Elena looked around, spotted an empty two-top by the window, and pointed. “There,” she said. They moved through the aisle, careful not to bump a waiter, and slid into the open table themselves. Elena placed her bag on the chair and pulled the menu toward her, then straightened the napkin on the table edge. A waiter passed and nodded without stopping. Elena lifted her hand briefly. “Cuando puedas,” she said. The waiter nodded again and kept moving. Elena and her brother settled in, unfolding their napkins and placing them on their laps. She set her phone face down and looked at the chalkboard specials on the wall, then told her brother, “You order the Albarino.” When the waiter came over a minute later, Elena ordered pulpo a la gallega and a plate of navajas. The waiter wrote it down and moved on, and Elena leaned back, watching the room while the cutlery clinked around them.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the party needs accessibility, the diners ask staff for guidance to select an appropriate table while still respecting the local habit.

GPT-5.2.name: Carmen Gil
GPT-5.2.story: Carmen entered a lively restaurant in Córdoba with her father, who used a cane and moved slowly on the tiled floor. The doorway was crowded, and a small sign suggested choosing an open table, but Carmen scanned the room for a clear path. She approached a waiter near the bar. “Perdona,” she said, gesturing gently toward her father. “We need a comfortable table, nearby and without a step, if possible.” The waiter nodded and looked around. “Yes, of course. There is one free over there,” he said, pointing to a table near the aisle with extra space. Carmen walked ahead, checking that the chairs could slide out without scraping against another table. “Does this work for you?” she asked her father. He tested the chair with one hand and nodded. Carmen helped him sit, then took the opposite seat and placed the cane beside the table where it wouldn’t trip anyone. When the waiter returned, Carmen said, “Thank you por indicarnos,” and ordered gazpacho and salmorejo to start. She folded her napkin and set the menu down, then leaned toward her father as he adjusted his posture and reached for his glass of water.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The diners choose their own table, but they do it with an entitled attitude and refuse to cooperate when staff need to adjust seating.

GPT-5.2.name: Jade Whitman
GPT-5.2.story: Jade walked into a packed tapas bar in Valencia where people stood at the counter and servers squeezed through carrying plates. She spotted an empty four-top near a column and headed straight for it. “We’re taking this,” she told her friends, dropping into a chair and setting her shopping bag on the table. A waiter approached and said, “Esa mesa la necesito juntar con la de al lado en un momento.” Jade didn’t move her bag. “We’re already sitting. That’s not my problem,” she replied, flipping the menu open with a sharp motion. The waiter glanced at the table next to them where a group was waiting to combine seats. “Por favor,” he said, reaching toward the extra chair. Jade pulled the chair closer to her side and said, “Don’t touch our stuff.” Her friend tried to stand and make space, but Jade waved her back down. “No, stay,” she said. The waiter paused, then spoke to another staff member and gestured toward the crowded entrance. Jade rolled her eyes and said, “It’s chaos here,” then snapped her fingers toward the bar. When the waiter returned later and asked again to shift the table slightly, Jade crossed her arms and stayed seated while the staff maneuvered around her.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The diners ignore staff instructions, take a reserved table on purpose, and treat the restaurant’s flow with disrespect.

GPT-5.2.name: Liam O'Connor
GPT-5.2.story: Liam and his friend stepped into a busy restaurant in Palma where a host stood by the entrance. “Please wait here,” the host said, holding up a hand while she checked a clipboard. Liam glanced past her and saw a large table with a small card that read “Reservado 21:00.” He nudged his friend. “That one,” he said, and walked around the host. “Sir, one moment,” she called after him. Liam ignored her and slid into the reserved table, pulling out a chair with a loud scrape. His friend sat too. Liam swept the reservation card aside onto the adjacent table and spread his arms over the space. “Finally,” he said, dropping his backpack on a chair. A waiter approached with a concerned look. “That table is reserved,” the waiter said. Liam waved him off. “We’re here now. Deal with it,” he replied, tapping the tabletop with his fingers. The host hurried over and repeated, “You cannot sit there.” Liam leaned back. “Find the reservation somewhere else,” he said, then signaled for menus by snapping his fingers once. The waiter looked toward the entrance as other diners waited behind them, and Liam kept his feet planted under the table.
